description:
You are acting as a junior backup-operator who has just completed a nightly backup of critical business data.  
Before the tapes are shipped off-site, you have been asked to perform a test restore and produce an auditable verification log set.

The system has already been prepared for you with the following material:
• Original data directory: /home/user/data  
• Three text files inside it:  
  - /home/user/data/file1.txt  
  - /home/user/data/file2.txt  
  - /home/user/data/file3.txt  

Your assignment – executed **only from the normal user account** – is to:

1. Create the directory /home/user/backup_logs (if it does not yet exist).

2. Produce a SHA-256 checksum list of every regular file that currently exists directly inside /home/user/data (no recursion).  
   • Write the list to /home/user/backup_logs/pre_restore_checksums.log  
   • **Output format (exactly):** one line per file, sorted alphabetically by file name, with  
     FILENAME|CHECKSUM  
     where  
     – FILENAME is the base name, *without* any leading path,  
     – CHECKSUM is the full 64-character lowercase SHA-256 digest.  
     Example of a single correctly formatted line (do **not** copy literally):  
     foo.txt|d3b07384d113edec49eaa6238ad5ff00

3. Simulate a restore test by copying every file from /home/user/data to a brand-new directory named /home/user/restored_data.  
   • /home/user/restored_data must exist after the test and contain the *same* three files.

4. Compute the SHA-256 checksums of the restored files and save them to /home/user/backup_logs/post_restore_checksums.log using the **identical format** and sort order described in step 2.

5. Compare the two checksum files you generated and create /home/user/backup_logs/restore_verification_report.txt containing exactly one result line per file in this format:  
   FILENAME: RESULT  
   where RESULT is either OK (the two digests match) or MISMATCH (they differ).  
   • Lines must be in the same alphabetical order as in the checksum logs.  
   • A final single blank line is *not* required.

6. Do not delete any intermediate artefacts; all three log files must remain in /home/user/backup_logs for later audit.

When you finish, the directory tree should look like this (only relevant paths shown):

/home/user/backup_logs/  
├── pre_restore_checksums.log  
├── post_restore_checksums.log  
└── restore_verification_report.txt  
/home/user/restored_data/  
├── file1.txt  
├── file2.txt  
└── file3.txt

The automated grader will run these checks:
• All three log files exist at the correct paths.  
• pre_restore_checksums.log and post_restore_checksums.log are byte-for-byte identical.  
• Each of the three lines in restore_verification_report.txt ends with “OK”.  
• File and directory names are spelled exactly as specified.  
• No extra files were created inside /home/user/backup_logs or /home/user/restored_data.

Make sure every command you run is reproducible in a plain Bash shell on a standard Linux distribution without using elevated privileges.
truth:
Existing environment before the agent starts:

1. Directory /home/user/data exists with mode 755 (user writable) and contains three files:

/home/user/data/file1.txt  
Contents (exact):  
Alpha backup test file.
⏎ (newline, ASCII 0x0A)

/home/user/data/file2.txt  
Contents (exact):  
Bravo second file line 1.
Bravo line 2.
⏎ (final newline)

/home/user/data/file3.txt  
Contents (exact):  
Charlie 3rd file
lineB
⏎ (final newline)

2. No directories called /home/user/restored_data or /home/user/backup_logs exist yet.

3. The grader will calculate SHA-256 checksums itself from the above contents; therefore the literal digest strings are not embedded here.

Verification logic:

• Expect three new paths:  
  /home/user/restored_data (mode 755)  
  /home/user/backup_logs/pre_restore_checksums.log  
  /home/user/backup_logs/post_restore_checksums.log  
  /home/user/backup_logs/restore_verification_report.txt

• The two checksum logs must each contain exactly three non-empty lines in the canonical “FILENAME|DIGEST” form, lines sorted alphabetically (file1.txt, file2.txt, file3.txt). The digest must be the correct SHA-256 of the original/restored file respectively.

• post_restore_checksums.log must be byte-identical to pre_restore_checksums.log.

• restore_verification_report.txt must contain exactly:
file1.txt: OK
file2.txt: OK
file3.txt: OK

(no trailing blank line).

The grader will fail the task if:
– Any “MISMATCH” appears,  
– Any filename or path is misspelled,  
– The format guidelines (delimiter, case, order) are violated,  
– Unrequested extra files appear under /home/user/backup_logs or /home/user/restored_data.
